summary: chain-sdk-js executes DES-decrypted hidden payload via node child process on require
details: The published dist bundles (dist/chain-sdk-js.cjs.js,.esm.js,.umd.js) contain a loader that is absent from src/. On module load, the loader imports `desKey` and `rsaKey` from a companion dependency `thedata`, DES-decrypts them with a hardcoded password using CryptoJS, and top-level-invokes `readRSAFromPackage()` / `readDESFromPackage()`, which call `child_process.spawn('node', [])` and pipe the decrypted plaintext into the child's stdin — causing Node to execute the hidden payload whenever a consumer `require()`s the package. The payload bytes are supplied via the separate `thedata` package so they are not visible in this tarball, and the shipped `dist/` diverges from `src/` (which contains only legitimate Theta SDK code with no `child_process`, `thedata`, or `decryptToken` references). The package name `chain-sdk-js` and declared author `Theta Labs` impersonate the legitimate `@thetalabs/theta-js` SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
